examples.title: Pseudocode: Static Allocation
examples.description: Demonstrates static allocation using an array
examples.code:
ALGORITHM StaticAllocation
INPUT: size
OUTPUT: array

BEGIN
 DECLARE array[size]
 FOR i FROM 0 TO size-1
 array[i] = i
 END FOR
 RETURN array
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates static allocation by declaring an array of a fixed size and initializing its elements.

examples.title: Python: Static Allocation
examples.description: Demonstrates static allocation using a Python list
examples.code:
def static_allocation(size):
 array = [i for i in range(size)]
 return array

# Example usage:
array = static_allocation(10)
print(array)
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates static allocation by creating a list of a fixed size and initializing its elements.

examples.title: Pseudocode: Linked Allocation
examples.description: Demonstrates linked allocation using a linked list
examples.code:
ALGORITHM LinkedAllocation
INPUT: value
OUTPUT: linked list

BEGIN
 DECLARE head = NULL
 DECLARE node = NEW NODE(value)
 IF head IS NULL
 head = node
 ELSE
 current = head
 WHILE current.next IS NOT NULL
 current = current.next
 END WHILE
 current.next = node
 END IF
 RETURN head
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates linked allocation by creating a new node and adding it to the end of a linked list.

examples.title: Python: Linked Allocation
examples.description: Demonstrates linked allocation using a Python linked list
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates linked allocation by creating a new node and adding it to the end of a linked list.
